item.title: Your SEO strategy is optimized for a search engine that no longer exists.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Google I/O made it official: AI-generated answers are now front and center in search, and most brands have almost no visibility into how AI is describing them to their customers. For anyone who has spent years building a strategy around 10 blue links, the rules just changed in a pretty significant way.  On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Rebecca […]

item.title: AI coding startup Cognition raises $1B at $25B pre-money valuation
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: As Cognition reaches $492 million in annualized revenue run rate, it more than doubled its valuation in eight months, it says.

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close today: Nominate a founder or submit your startup
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Today is the final day to apply or nominate a startup for Startup Battlefield 200. Once the clock strikes 11:59 p.m. PT, the window closes on your chance to compete for $100,000 in equity-free funding, gain global visibility, connect directly with investors, and launch on the TechCrunch Disrupt stage.

item.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket savings end in 3 days
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: There are only 3 days left to save up to $410 on your ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Early Bird pricing ends May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT, and once the deadline passes, ticket prices increase. If you plan to attend one of the most influential gatherings in tech this year, now is the time to lock in your pass before rates go up again.

item.title: SOND, a sleep tech startup from Bose’s former head of sleep, exits stealth with $7M
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: SOND introduced its debut product: Dreambuds, a closed-loop, in-ear system that captures 12 physiological signals from the wearer, then acts on them in real time to help consumers get better sleep.

item.title: Tech CEOs are apparently suffering from AI psychosis
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: "CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis," Box CEO Aaron Levie opines. Maybe that explains the almost religious belief in AI productivity gains.

item.title: Airbnb-backed WeRoad raises $58M to take its group travel platform to the US
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: WeRoad, the Milan-based group travel startup, has raised a $58 million Series C round led by Airbnb as it prepares for its first major expansion outside Europe. The funding brings the company’s total capital raised to roughly $100 million and will finance WeRoad’s push into the U.S., beginning with Austin. The new investment reflects a […]

item.title: What we’re looking for in Startup Battlefield 2026, and how to apply in time for the May 27 deadline
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: Startup Battlefield applications are due tomorrow, so now's the time to put the finishing touches on your submission!

item.title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: OpenRouter has raised a $113 million Series B led by CapitalG. Its 5x growth in usage over six months indicates the multi-AI-model future is here.

item.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket rates end May 29
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Save up to $410 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass before prices increase on May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here to join the tech epicenter in San Francisco.

item.title: Amazon fulfillment competitor Stord raises $250M at $3B valuation
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Stord offers a network of physical warehouses and inventory management software for e-commerce. It bills itself as a sort of anti-Amazon, giving brands "the speed to compete" while still owning their customer relationships.

item.title: What ClickUp’s mass layoff tells us about the future of work
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The nine-year-old startup is replacing hundreds of employees with thousands of AI agents.

item.title: The pitch trick that helped an esports startup raise $20M when VCs only wanted AI
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Earlier this year, Lucra Sports founder and CEO Dylan Robbins did something that no one else has ever done. And he shared several secrets on how he did it.

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close in days: Apply before May 27
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is May 27. This is your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100,000. Apply now.

item.title: 5 days left: Save up to $410 on TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 passes before prices increase
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Early Bird savings for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 in San Francisco end May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register now to save up to $410 before prices increase.

item.title: SolarSquare in talks to raise up to $60M as India’s rooftop solar market draws major VC interest
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: SolarSquare could be valued at up to $500 million in the financing expected to close next month.

item.title: Peec, one of Berlin’s rising startups, more than doubled annualized revenue in months to $10M, sources say
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Peec, which helps brands track their presence in AI searches, offers proof of a key trend among European startups.

item.title: How VCs and founders use inflated ‘ARR’ to crown AI startups
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Some AI startups are stretching traditional revenue metrics when talking about progress publicly. And their investors are fully aware.

item.title: Elon Musk can’t hear you over the sound of his $1.75 trillion IPO
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Kirsten Korosec, Sean O'Kane, Anthony Ha
item.description: The SpaceX S-1 is finally here, and the story it tells goes way further than rockets. The filing runs to 36 pages of risk factors alone, and the numbers inside match the ambition: a $28 trillion total addressable market, a pay package tied to establishing a Mars colony, and a valuation target that would make it the largest IPO in American […]

item.title: This young startup is taking on a fragrance industry that hasn’t changed in almost half a century
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Fragrance tech company Patina announced Thursday a $2 million raise from investors, including Betaworks and True Ventures.
